settings.title: A Sandstone Patio Built for Long Evenings
settings.summary: We laid a generous sandstone patio with a planted pergola for a family who entertain outdoors.
settings.description:
The Okafors hosted big family gatherings but only had a cramped slab patio that sat half their guests. People ended up spilling onto the lawn or staying indoors, which was not what they wanted from a garden they used for entertaining.

What they needed

The main ask was simple: enough room to seat the whole family comfortably at one table, with a connection back to the kitchen so food and drink did not have to travel far. They also wanted some shade and some shelter, without building anything that felt heavy in the space.

What we built

We laid a wide riven sandstone terrace stepped down from the kitchen doors, so the level change reads as a generous step rather than an awkward drop. The riven surface gives grip and a bit of texture, and the size means the whole family now fits around one table with room to move behind the chairs.

Along one side we framed the terrace with an oak pergola and trained a wisteria and a grapevine up it. Even in its first season the structure has started to do its job, and over time the climbers will close the canopy and soften the timber.

  • Wide riven sandstone terrace stepped down from the kitchen
  • Oak pergola framing one side of the seating area
  • Wisteria and grapevine trained to grow over the structure

The result

The terrace seats their whole family now and holds warmth into the evening, so gatherings carry on long after the sun has gone. The pergola already throws useful shade by midsummer, and it will only get better as the planting matures.
